title: Price quality relationships in fodder trading in Niger with special regards to comparison of cowpea & groundnut haulms with concentrates, collected shrubs and grasses
abstract: Investigations of price-quality relationships in feed and fodder marketing can yield crucial information about economic benefits from livestock production, specific feed demand and supply, opportunities for engaging in feed and fodder production and transaction and least cost diet designs.
funders: Dryland Systems, Dryland Cereals, Gender component Grain Legumes and Africa Rising
